title: Cup of tea (polish way)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a4omh","text":"This protocol is part of the lesson within the FAIR in (biological) practice Carpentry course. Here we explore the perfect protocol to brew tea the polish way.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":47,"length":29}],"data":{}},{"key":"a2uvk","text":"Tea in Poland is served in transparent glasses to admire the colour of the tea and judge its \"strength\". Tea is never served with milk but often with a bit of lemon.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4hs3l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5rfrs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"27B24320A68411EBA77DE362B6D911DF","url":"https://carpentries-incubator.github.io/fair-bio-practice/"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5c9st","text":"Make sure the kettle is cleaned and the bottom of the kettle is free of lime-scale.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bpnro","text":"Should there be lime-scale, add small amount of 5% vinegar just covering the base to the kettle and leave over-night, or add about half a cm of 5% vinegar and boil the kettle. The latter should get rid of the lime-scale right away. Any type of malt-vinegar should do the job.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1l4vq","text":"Black tea is a liquid so elevated consumption might lead to increased bladder activity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2suc2","text":"Ideally soft tap water, Scottish tap water is perfect","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9r6u5","text":"Andrew James 1.8L kettle","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ghmp","text":"Transparent 250ml glass or mug","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ac8k8","text":"Black tea - Brodies Tea bag, 2g","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a3t6l","text":"sugar","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5krg2","text":"lemon","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d9cef","text":"tea spoon","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fill the kettle with over 250mL of water, or until your min fill line and boil the water.
In the meantime prepare your tea bag, unpack it and place it in the transparent glass (or a mug). If the paper tag is present, position it outside of the mug.

Once the water is boiled, instantly pour the hot water into the mug. Hint: Ensure you fix the tag on the outside of the mug with your finger, to avoid falling in of the string + tag of the tea bag. Otherwise removal of the tea bag becomes more difficult.

Brew the tea for 1 to 3 min, keep an eye on the changing color you want to interrupt brewing once it reaches light brown, like oak wood. Don't wait too long till it becomes dark brown that is considered a too strong tea in Poland.
Use the string/tag to remove the tea bag. Discard the tea bag in your compost for most appropriate recycling .

Wait 5 minutes and either squizz in few drops of lemon or insert a lemon slice. See how the color changes to bright brown or yellow. Hint: Never add lemon to boiling hot tea as it destroys the natural vitamin C.
creator: Tomasz Zieliński
